evals.prompt: Continue the backlog-burndown campaign in this repo. There is an active handover in .claude/handovers/ and a campaign tracker (a Backlog.md doc). Resolve the next queued backlog issue and prepare everything so a fresh session can continue the campaign afterward.
evals.expected_output: task-1 resolved Done with AC evidence; work done on feature/task-1 then ff-merged to main, pushed, branch pruned local+remote; tracker cursor advanced to task-2 with a session-log entry; old handover archived, fresh handover written pointing at task-2.
files: sandbox: git repo + bare origin + backlog project with task-1/task-2, tracker doc-1 (cursor task-1), active handover
assertions: task-1 is Done with its acceptance criterion checked
work is merged to main and the working tree is clean
a feature branch was used (reflog shows checkout to feature/*)
feature branches pruned locally and remotely
session log entry appended to tracker
old handover archived and a fresh handover exists mentioning task-2

evals.prompt: Set up a backlog-burndown campaign in this repo so that future sessions can each resolve one backlog issue and hand over to the next session. The open issues are task-1 and task-2; queue order task-1 then task-2 — I confirm that order now. Set up whatever durable tracking the campaign needs and prepare the first session handoff. Do NOT resolve any issue yet — setup only.
evals.expected_output: Tracker doc created (cursor task-1, queue in confirmed order, confirmation recorded); .claude/handovers/ gitignored + archive/handovers/ created; tracker committed; first handover written pointing at task-1; no issue actually resolved.
files: sandbox: git repo + bare origin + backlog project with task-1/task-2, no tracker, no handover
assertions: a campaign tracker doc exists with cursor at task-1
queue records both issues in the confirmed order
no issue was resolved (both To Do, typo intact, no LICENSE)
a first handover exists pointing at task-1
tracker is committed and the working tree is clean
